Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Decade of Slug 668 upvotes, 64 comments
  2. More than 135 open hardware devices flashable with your own firmware 280 upvotes, 34 comments
  3. A tale about fixing eBPF spinlock issues in the Linux kernel 122 upvotes, 13 comments
  4. Nightingale – open-source karaoke app that works with any song on your computer 209 upvotes, 34 comments
  5. Unsloth Studio 331 upvotes, 63 comments
  6. Write up of my homebrew CPU build 125 upvotes, 20 comments
  7. Mistral AI Releases Forge 586 upvotes, 143 comments
  8. (Media over QUIC) on a Boat 56 upvotes, 8 comments
  9. JPEG Compression 297 upvotes, 73 comments
  10. Show HN: Sub-millisecond VM sandboxes using CoW memory forking 211 upvotes, 54 comments
  11. Microsoft's 'unhackable' Xbox One has been hacked by 'Bliss' 729 upvotes, 272 comments
  12. Celebrating Tony Hoare's mark on computer science 66 upvotes, 17 comments
  13. The pleasures of poor product design 168 upvotes, 59 comments
  14. It Took Me 30 Years to Solve This VFX Problem – Green Screen Problem [video] 264 upvotes, 103 comments
  15. How the Eon Team Produced a Virtual Embodied Fly 45 upvotes, 12 comments
  16. Using calculus to do number theory 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  17. Electron microscopy shows ‘mouse bite’ defects in semiconductors 88 upvotes, 29 comments
  18. Show HN: Pgit – A Git-like CLI backed by PostgreSQL 77 upvotes, 28 comments
  19. Pandas Exercises for Data Analysis (Interactive) 40 upvotes, 12 comments
  20. Tech hobbyist makes shoulder-mounted guided missile prototype with $96 in parts 24 upvotes, 6 comments
  21. Rob Pike's 5 Rules of Programming 316 upvotes, 156 comments
  22. Get Shit Done: A meta-prompting, context engineering and spec-driven dev system 373 upvotes, 191 comments
  23. Ryugu asteroid samples contain all DNA and RNA building blocks 275 upvotes, 145 comments
  24. Have a fucking website 598 upvotes, 342 comments
  25. Python 3.15's JIT is now back on track 419 upvotes, 242 comments
  26. Why AI systems don't learn – On autonomous learning from cognitive science 150 upvotes, 87 comments
  27. Pardoned for Fraud, a CEO Mounts His Comeback: 'We Can Trust You Now' 16 upvotes, 13 comments
  28. Animation 10k Starlink Satellites 30 upvotes, 35 comments
  29. Measuring progress toward AGI: A cognitive framework 56 upvotes, 94 comments
  30. Ndea (YC W26) is hiring a symbolic RL search guidance lead 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 10:00pm, 18 March 2026. Source here.
